1.title: The last book of the Old Testament
1.text: 'Malachi' means 'my messenger.' His book closes the Old Testament, and after him comes some four centuries of prophetic silence before the New Testament opens.

1.question: In Malachi 1:2-3, whom does the Lord say he loved, and whom he hated?
options: He loved Jacob and hated Esau
He loved Esau and hated Jacob

1.question: In Malachi 1:8, what kind of animals were the priests offering in sacrifice?
options: The blind, the lame, and the sick

1.title: A name great among the nations
1.text: Verse 11 declares that from the rising of the sun to its going down the Lord's name shall be great 'among the Gentiles' — a striking promise that his worship would reach beyond Israel.

2.question: According to Malachi 2:4-5, with whom did the Lord make a covenant of life and peace?

2.template: for he is the {0} of the Lord of hosts

2.question: Malachi 2:10 asks a question to rebuke the people's treachery. What is it?
options: Who can find a virtuous woman?
Have we not all one father? Has not one God created us?
How long will you halt between two opinions?
Where were you when I laid the foundations?

2.title: The Lord hates 'putting away'
2.text: Verse 16 says the Lord 'hateth putting away' — that is, unjust divorce. Malachi treats marriage as a covenant, tying faithfulness to one's spouse to faithfulness to God.

2.question: In Malachi 2:17, how have the people wearied the Lord?
options: By saying that those who do evil are good in his sight

3.title: Prepare the way before me
3.text: Verse 1 — 'Behold, I will send my messenger, and he shall prepare the way before me' — is quoted in the Gospels and applied to John the Baptist, who prepared the way for the Lord.

3.question: In Malachi 3:2-3, the coming Lord is compared to a refiner's fire who purifies whom?
In Malachi 3:6, what does the Lord say about himself, so that the sons of Jacob are not consumed?
options: 'I am the Lord, I change not'

3.question: In Malachi 3:10, what does the Lord promise if the people bring all the tithes into the storehouse?
options: To send rain in due season
To open the windows of heaven and pour out a blessing

3.question: According to Malachi 3:12, what will all nations call the obedient people?
Malachi 3:18 says the people shall return and discern the difference between whom?

4.title: The Sun of righteousness
4.text: Verse 2 promises that to those who fear the Lord's name 'the Sun of righteousness' shall arise 'with healing in his wings' — one of the Old Testament's brightest images of hope.

4.template: I will send you {0} the prophet

4.question: According to Malachi 4:6, what will the prophet do before the great and dreadful day of the Lord?
options: Turn the heart of the fathers to the children, and the children to the fathers
